prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of furan performed with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fit RI, PySCF), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry used as-is from the supplied furan.xyz (9 atoms: O, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H); no re-optimization. Fukui indices computed via finite-difference Mulliken charges (N, N+1, N-1 states). Integrity: trustworthy=true, all checks passed (sum(f+)=1.0000, sum(f-)=1.0000). The most electrophilic atom is atom 5 (C, one of the two alpha-carbons flanking oxygen) with f+ = 1.9242931, consistent with furan's well-known electrophilic aromatic substitution reactivity at the 2- and 5-positions. Negative Fukui values on O and H atoms are a known Mulliken-scheme artifact. No engine warnings reported. Plot at: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260706-175706_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz
